failure_modes.text: False pattern recognition can make teams force a hidden-information story onto a problem that is really about incentives, execution, or design.
failure_modes.note: The raw file is proxy-derived, so this chunk stays close to the explicit anti-pattern language.
failure_modes.mitigation: Apply multiple frames before settling on an adverse selection explanation; test whether incentives, execution failure, or design error better explain the observed outcome.

failure_modes.text: Framework fetishism can hide the assumptions embedded in a screening story and make the selection diagnosis feel truer than it is.
failure_modes.mitigation: Spell out all implicit assumptions in the screening story and pressure-test each against the outside view before acting on the diagnosis.

failure_modes.text: Assuming asymmetry without verifying the selection mechanism can misdiagnose the failure entirely.
failure_modes.source_quote: Assuming Asymmetry Without Verifying the Selection Mechanism
failure_modes.mitigation: Verify the specific mechanism of information asymmetry rather than assuming it exists; structure the problem into logical parts before jumping to the adverse selection explanation.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe to accept this selection story, and which of those assumptions has not been tested yet?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe to accept a particular thesis or viewpoint?

premortem_questions.text: If someone came to me asking about this decision, what would I need to know before claiming hidden quality or hidden risk is the driver?
premortem_questions.source_quote: If someone came to me asking my opinion about this kind of decision, what would I need to know to give good advice?
premortem_questions.note: Specialized conservatively from the raw outside-view check to the adverse-selection use case.

heuristics.text: Structure the problem before analysis so screening, signaling, or filtering mechanisms are explicit rather than implied.
heuristics.source_quote: Problem Structuring: Rigorously frame a question or hypothesis before complex analysis

heuristics.text: Anchor to the outside view before treating one side's private-information story as the dominant explanation.
heuristics.source_quote: Outside View Anchor: Anchor decisions first to the outside view

heuristics.text: Test the case with multiple models or frames so hidden-information reasoning competes with other plausible explanations.
heuristics.source_quote: Multiple Models/Frames: Break down complex problems using multiple frameworks or mental models

curation_notes.summary: This source is proxy-derived rather than explicitly named around adverse selection, so the chunks stay tightly anchored to verification, framing, and anti-misdiagnosis content.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor material that treated adverse selection as more explicitly sourced than the local raw file supports.
open_questions: This is one of the more normalization-dependent Wave 2 files because the raw text frames the mechanism through proxy decision-hygiene content.

allies: The raw allies section is useful future relation material around first principles, systems thinking, and fast-slow thinking.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is rich on cognitive biases, curse of knowledge, and emotional thinking as future relation material.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against algorithmic-thinking, systems-thinking, and confirmation-bias.
